coverage.notes: Previous run crashed due to --coverage-output being a directory instead of a file path. Fixed: all source files now instrumented. Generated .g.cs files at 11-15% (expected — tests call Run() directly, not through generated pipeline). ICryptoProvider.cs is an interface, no coverable lines.

findings.issue: Verify.Run() catches FormatException from Convert.FromHexString(Hash) but Hash=null throws ArgumentNullException, which is unhandled. Violates 'behavior methods never throw' contract.
findings.impact: If a .pr file or source generator passes null Hash, Verify throws an unhandled exception instead of returning Data.FromError(). This is the exact bug pattern the auditor has flagged as major on other modules.
findings.suggestion: Either widen catch to include ArgumentNullException, or add a null guard before Convert.FromHexString. Add a test with Hash = null! to prove the fix.

findings.issue: No test for Verify with Hash = "" (empty string). Behavior is correct (returns false) but undocumented by tests.
findings.suggestion: Optional: add a test documenting empty hash behavior

findings.issue: All 9 registry tests use ICryptoProvider as type key. No test verifies type isolation (registering type A doesn't affect type B).
findings.impact: Near zero — ConcurrentDictionary<Type, object> guarantees isolation by framework
findings.suggestion: Optional: add one test with two different interface types

findings.issue: No test for mixed-case Algorithm names (e.g., 'Keccak256'). Hash normalizes to lowercase in HashedData, but Verify passes raw Algorithm to provider.
findings.impact: Low — DefaultProvider normalizes internally. Custom providers might not.
findings.suggestion: Optional: test with Algorithm = 'KECCAK256' to document case handling

summary: v1 findings all resolved. Fresh-eyes analysis found 1 major: Verify.Run() can throw unhandled ArgumentNullException when Hash is null (catch only handles FormatException). This violates the 'behavior methods never throw' contract. 3 minor edge cases. All previous test quality issues fixed — serialization anchor works, algorithm verification works, provider registry fully tested.
